Finding.description: One piece of evidence, grounded in the rendered input facts.
Finding.title: Finding

json_schema.description: Shared response shape for the source investigators.
json_schema.title: InvestigatorResponse

request.system:
You are one specialist in an evidence-first incident investigation pipeline
for a SaaS engineering team. You receive validated facts extracted from an
offline incident package - never raw files, never live systems.

Non-negotiable rules:
- Cite only information present in the input. Never invent timestamps,
  services, signals, or values.
- Copy timestamps exactly as they appear in the input (ISO-8601).
- Every finding's interpretation states what the data shows and how strongly;
  say "consistent with" rather than "proves" when evidence is indirect.
- You investigate and describe. You never recommend executing actions.
- If the input is insufficient to answer something in your remit, record it
  in `gaps` instead of guessing.
- Findings must be specific enough that another engineer could verify each
  one against the same input in seconds.

Role: metrics investigator.
Input: metric series with pre-incident baselines, plus the incident window.
The documented deviation rule (docs/assumptions.md): a value is anomalous at
>= 2x baseline or <= 0.5x baseline; with a zero baseline any nonzero value
is anomalous.

Report as findings, each tied to a concrete series and timestamp:
- for each series that deviates: when it first crossed, how far it peaked
  relative to baseline, and whether it recovered inside the window
- ordering between deviating series (which moved first)
- series that stayed at baseline - unaffected services are evidence too
- suspicious shapes the rule misses (steady climb, step change, plateau)
Do not speculate about causes in other sources; describe metric behavior.

response.text: {"findings": [{"interpretation": "booking-service p95_latency_ms first crossed 2x baseline (900 ms >= 2×450) at 2026-06-01T14:30:00+00:00. Latency peaked at 3300.0 ms (7.3× baseline) at 2026-06-01T14:50:00+00:00. No recovery observed; remained elevated at 3200 ms at window end 2026-06-01T15:00:00+00:00.", "timestamp": "2026-06-01T14:30:00+00:00", "service": "booking-service", "signal": "p95_latency_ms", "value": 900.0}, {"interpretation": "booking-service error_rate_pct first crossed 2x baseline (0.6% >= 2×0.3) at 2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00. Error rate peaked at 4.8% (16× baseline) at 2026-06-01T14:40:00+00:00. No recovery observed; remained elevated at 4.8% at window end 2026-06-01T15:00:00+00:00.", "timestamp": "2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00", "service": "booking-service", "signal": "error_rate_pct", "value": 0.7}, {"interpretation": "payment-service p95_latency_ms first crossed 2x baseline (410 ms >= 2×220) at 2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00, 5 minutes after booking-service error_rate_pct deviated. Latency peaked at 2450.0 ms (11.1× baseline) at 2026-06-01T14:50:00+00:00. No recovery observed; remained at 2380 ms at window end 2026-06-01T15:00:00+00:00.", "timestamp": "2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00", "service": "payment-service", "signal": "p95_latency_ms", "value": 410.0}, {"interpretation": "appointments-db cpu_pct first crossed 2x baseline (76% >= 2×38) at 2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00. CPU utilization peaked at 93.0% (2.4× baseline) at 2026-06-01T14:50:00+00:00. No recovery observed; remained at 92% at window end 2026-06-01T15:00:00+00:00.", "timestamp": "2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00", "service": "appointments-db", "signal": "cpu_pct", "value": 55.0}, {"interpretation": "booking-queue queue_depth first crossed 2x baseline (24 messages >= 2×12) at 2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00. Queue depth peaked at 545.0 messages (45.4× baseline) at 2026-06-01T15:00:00+00:00. No recovery observed; remained at elevated plateau.", "timestamp": "2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00", "service": "booking-queue", "signal": "queue_depth", "value": 35.0}, {"interpretation": "notifications-service p95_latency_ms remained within baseline range (118–124 ms, all <240 ms) throughout incident window. No deviation or anomaly detected.", "timestamp": null, "service": "notifications-service", "signal": "p95_latency_ms", "value": null}, {"interpretation": "Temporal ordering: booking-service error_rate_pct and payment-service p95_latency_ms first deviated at 2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00 (tied). booking-service p95_latency_ms deviated 5 minutes later at 2026-06-01T14:30:00+00:00. appointments-db cpu_pct and booking-queue queue_depth deviated at 2026-06-01T14:25:00+00:00 (tied with first group). All deviated series show sustained elevation with no recovery through window end.", "timestamp": null, "service": null, "signal": null, "value": null}, {"interpretation": "Shape characteristic: all five deviated series exhibit a sharp acceleration phase between 2026-06-01T14:20:00+00:00 and 2026-06-01T14:35:00+00:00 (exponential or step-like climb), followed by plateau/leveling-off from 2026-06-01T14:40:00+00:00 onward. This sustained plateau pattern is consistent with a system reaching saturation or queue saturation rather than transient spike.", "timestamp": null, "service": null, "signal": null, "value": null}], "gaps": ["No metric data after 2026-06-01T15:00:00+00:00; cannot determine whether deviations persisted beyond that point or eventually recovered.", "No supporting logs, traces, or deployment events provided to correlate with metric deviation timeline.", "No metrics from upstream services (e.g., API gateway, load balancer) to establish whether latency escalation originated in booking/payment services or propagated inbound."], "reasoning": "Five of six metric series deviated sharply during a 5-minute window (14:25–14:30). booking-service error_rate_pct crossed the 2× threshold first at 14:25; payment-service p95_latency_ms followed at the same moment, suggesting a common triggering event. appointments-db cpu_pct and booking-queue queue_depth also crossed at 14:25, implying load accumulation. booking-service p95_latency_ms joined at 14:30. All deviations escalated through 14:40 and then plateaued at elevated levels, consistent with a backend reaching saturation. notifications-service remained unaffected throughout, suggesting the incident was localized to the booking/payment request path and appointment database. No recovery was observed in any deviated series within the window. The sustained plateau shape (rather than spike-and-dip) indicates the system remained in a degraded state at window end."}
